name: Service Availability
description: Percentage of agreed service time that a service is actually available for use. The primary measure of service uptime performance.
measurement_guidance: Measure from user perspective. Exclude agreed maintenance windows. Track per service and per business-critical period.

name: Mean Time Between Failures (MTBF)
description: Average time between service failures. Higher MTBF indicates more reliable service. Calculated as total uptime divided by number of failures.
measurement_guidance: Count only unplanned failures. Measure per service or component. Track trend over rolling 12-month period.

name: Mean Time to Restore Service (MTRS)
description: Average time taken to restore a service after a failure. Includes detection, diagnosis, repair, and recovery time. Lower MTRS indicates better incident response capabilities.
measurement_guidance: Measure from failure detection to full service restoration. Differentiate by severity. Include all contributing downtime.

name: Planned Downtime Percentage
description: Percentage of total time that a service is unavailable due to planned maintenance. Measures how well maintenance windows are managed and minimized.
measurement_guidance: Track all planned maintenance windows. Compare against customer-agreed maintenance schedules.

name: Unplanned Downtime Minutes
description: Total minutes of unplanned service downtime within a reporting period. A raw measure of service disruption impact.
measurement_guidance: Track per service and per severity. Report monthly. Compare against SLA downtime budget.

name: Service Availability During Business Hours
description: Availability measured only during defined business-critical hours rather than 24x7. More relevant for services that have peak usage periods.
measurement_guidance: Define business hours per service. May vary by time zone and customer segment.

name: Component Availability
description: Availability of individual infrastructure components (servers, networks, databases) that support services. Enables root cause identification for service-level availability issues.
measurement_guidance: Monitor per component. Aggregate to service level using dependency mapping. Include in capacity planning.

name: Redundancy and Failover Success Rate
description: Percentage of failover events that successfully maintained service without user-visible impact. Measures the effectiveness of high-availability architectures.
measurement_guidance: Test failover regularly. Record all automatic and manual failover events. Track time to failover.

name: Application Response Time
description: Time taken for a service to respond to a user request. Measured at various percentiles (p50, p90, p99) to understand typical and worst-case performance.
measurement_guidance: Measure end-to-end from user perspective. Use synthetic monitoring and real user monitoring. Track by endpoint/transaction.

name: Transaction Throughput
description: Number of transactions processed per unit of time. Measures the system's ability to handle load and deliver results within acceptable timeframes.
measurement_guidance: Define what constitutes a transaction per service. Measure during peak and off-peak. Alert on throughput degradation.

name: Page Load Time
description: Time for a web page to fully render in the user's browser. Critical for user experience in web-based services. Measured using real user monitoring or synthetic testing.
measurement_guidance: Use Core Web Vitals. Measure from representative locations. Segment by device type and connection speed.

name: API Response Time
description: Time for an API endpoint to return a response. Measured at the server side (excluding network latency) or end-to-end including network transit time.
measurement_guidance: Measure per endpoint. Track error responses separately. Alert on percentile degradation.

name: Batch Processing Completion Time
description: Time taken to complete scheduled batch processing jobs within agreed processing windows. Critical for data processing, reporting, and integration workloads.
measurement_guidance: Track per job and per processing window. Monitor trend to identify jobs approaching window limits.

name: Error Rate
description: Percentage of requests that result in errors (4xx, 5xx HTTP status codes, application errors). Measures service reliability from the user's perspective.
measurement_guidance: Distinguish between client errors (4xx) and server errors (5xx). Track by endpoint. Alert on sudden increases.

name: Database Query Response Time
description: Time for database queries to execute and return results. Includes both read and write operations. Critical for application performance.
measurement_guidance: Monitor per query type. Identify slow queries. Track query plan changes. Alert on degradation.

name: CPU Utilization
description: Percentage of CPU capacity being used. High sustained utilization can indicate capacity constraints, while very low utilization may indicate over-provisioning.
measurement_guidance: Measure peak and average. Track per server and per service. Forecast using trend analysis.

name: Memory Utilization
description: Percentage of available memory being used. Sustained high memory utilization can lead to performance degradation, swapping, and out-of-memory conditions.
measurement_guidance: Distinguish between committed and cached memory. Monitor for memory leaks. Alert on sustained high usage.

name: Storage Utilization
description: Percentage of storage capacity being used. Monitors disk space, database size, and object storage to prevent capacity exhaustion.
measurement_guidance: Track growth rate. Forecast days to capacity limit. Include all storage tiers (SSD, HDD, object).

name: Network Bandwidth Utilization
description: Percentage of available network bandwidth being consumed. High utilization can cause latency, packet loss, and service degradation.
measurement_guidance: Measure at key network boundaries. Track peak vs average. Monitor for burst patterns.

name: Capacity Forecast Accuracy
description: Accuracy of capacity forecasts compared to actual capacity consumption. Measures the effectiveness of capacity planning processes.
measurement_guidance: Compare quarterly forecasts to actuals. Track by resource type. Identify systematic over/under-estimation.

name: Mean Time to Resolve (MTTR)
description: Average time from incident creation to resolution. The primary measure of incident management effectiveness. Should be tracked by priority level.
measurement_guidance: Measure by priority. Exclude wait-on-customer time. Track trend monthly. Compare to SLA targets.

name: First Contact Resolution Rate
description: Percentage of incidents resolved on first contact with the service desk without escalation to second or third-line support.
measurement_guidance: Define what qualifies as first-contact. Include phone, chat, and email channels. Track per category.

name: Incident Backlog
description: Number of open incidents at any point in time, segmented by priority and age. Growing backlogs indicate capacity or capability issues in incident management.
measurement_guidance: Track total and by priority. Monitor aging (incidents open > 30 days). Alert on growing trend.

name: Incident SLA Compliance Rate
description: Percentage of incidents resolved within their SLA target response and resolution times. The primary compliance measure for incident management agreements.
measurement_guidance: Measure response SLA and resolution SLA separately. Track by priority and by service.

name: Mean Time to Detect (MTTD)
description: Average time from when a failure occurs to when it is detected. Lower MTTD indicates better monitoring and alerting capabilities.
measurement_guidance: Measure from actual failure time (if determinable) to first alert/ticket. Track improvement with monitoring enhancements.

name: Incident Reopened Rate
description: Percentage of resolved incidents that are reopened because the resolution was ineffective. High reopen rates indicate quality issues in diagnosis or resolution.
measurement_guidance: Define reopen criteria (e.g., same issue within 7 days). Track by support group and category.

name: Major Incident Count
description: Number of major incidents (highest severity) per reporting period. Tracks the frequency of significant service disruptions.
measurement_guidance: Define major incident criteria clearly. Track per service and overall. Analyze trends and patterns.

name: Customer Satisfaction (Incident)
description: Customer satisfaction score for incident handling, typically collected through post-incident surveys. Measures the quality of the incident experience from the customer perspective.
measurement_guidance: Survey after each resolved incident. Track response rate. Analyze comments for improvement themes.

name: Change Success Rate
description: Percentage of changes implemented successfully without causing incidents. The primary quality measure for change management.
measurement_guidance: Define success criteria per change type. Track failed and backed-out changes. Analyze failure patterns.

name: Emergency Change Percentage
description: Percentage of total changes that are emergency changes. High emergency change rates indicate reactive operations and potentially inadequate planning.
measurement_guidance: Define emergency change criteria strictly. Track trend. Analyze root causes of emergency changes.

name: Change Lead Time
description: Time from change request submission to successful implementation. Measures the speed and efficiency of the change management process.
measurement_guidance: Track by change type (standard, normal, emergency). Identify bottlenecks in approval process.

name: Change-Related Incidents
description: Number of incidents caused by changes to the production environment. Measures the stability impact of change activities.
measurement_guidance: Correlate incidents with recent changes. Track by change type and team. Use for change risk assessment improvement.

name: Change Backlog Volume
description: Number of approved but not yet implemented changes. Growing backlogs may indicate capacity constraints or process bottlenecks.
measurement_guidance: Track aging of backlog items. Alert when backlog exceeds threshold. Monitor capacity vs demand.

name: Service Request Fulfillment Time
description: Average time to fulfill a service request from submission to completion. Measures the efficiency of request fulfillment processes.
measurement_guidance: Track by request type. Differentiate automated vs manual fulfillment. Identify opportunities for automation.

name: Service Request SLA Compliance
description: Percentage of service requests fulfilled within their agreed SLA target time. Measures adherence to service request commitments.
measurement_guidance: Measure by request category. Track against category-specific SLAs. Report monthly.

name: Request Automation Rate
description: Percentage of service requests fulfilled through automated workflows without manual intervention. Higher automation rates indicate process maturity and efficiency.
measurement_guidance: Identify automatable request types. Track automation adoption trend. Calculate cost savings from automation.

name: Service Request Volume
description: Total number of service requests submitted per reporting period. Tracks demand patterns and resource requirements for fulfillment teams.
measurement_guidance: Track trend over time. Segment by category. Correlate with business events. Use for capacity planning.

name: Self-Service Adoption Rate
description: Percentage of service requests submitted through self-service channels (portal, chatbot) rather than traditional channels (phone, email).
measurement_guidance: Track by channel. Measure self-service satisfaction separately. Identify barriers to adoption.

name: Problems Identified
description: Number of problems identified through reactive analysis of incidents and proactive trend analysis per reporting period.
measurement_guidance: Track reactive vs proactive problems separately. Higher proactive count indicates mature problem management.

name: Problem Resolution Time
description: Average time from problem identification to permanent resolution. Longer times are expected compared to incidents as problems require root cause analysis.
measurement_guidance: Track by priority and category. Separate workaround time from permanent fix time. Monitor aging problems.

name: Known Error Database Coverage
description: Number of known errors documented with workarounds. Measures the knowledge available to speed incident resolution.
measurement_guidance: Track growth over time. Monitor workaround usage by service desk. Update when permanent fixes are deployed.

name: Recurring Incident Reduction
description: Percentage reduction in recurring incidents after problem resolution. Measures the effectiveness of problem management in preventing repeat incidents.
measurement_guidance: Compare incident counts before and after problem resolution. Track per problem. Report cumulatively.

name: Problem Backlog
description: Number of open problems at any point in time. Growing backlogs indicate insufficient capacity for root cause analysis and resolution.
measurement_guidance: Track by priority and age. Alert on problems open longer than 90 days. Monitor trend monthly.

name: Security Incident Count
description: Number of security incidents detected per reporting period, categorized by severity. Tracks the security threat landscape and incident response workload.
measurement_guidance: Classify by severity and type. Track trend. Compare to industry benchmarks. Report to management.

name: Vulnerability Remediation Time
description: Average time from vulnerability discovery to remediation, segmented by severity (critical, high, medium, low).
measurement_guidance: Track by severity. Measure from scan detection to verified remediation. Alert on overdue vulnerabilities.

name: Patch Compliance Rate
description: Percentage of systems that are patched within the agreed timeframe for security patches. Measures the effectiveness of vulnerability management.
measurement_guidance: Track by OS type and criticality. Measure within SLA windows. Identify systems that are consistently behind.

name: Security Awareness Training Completion
description: Percentage of employees who have completed required security awareness training within the defined period.
measurement_guidance: Track completion annually. Measure phishing simulation click rates. Segment by department.

name: Mean Time to Contain Security Incident
description: Average time from security incident detection to containment of the threat. Measures the speed and effectiveness of security incident response.
measurement_guidance: Measure from detection to confirmed containment. Track by incident type. Practice through tabletop exercises.

name: Recovery Time Objective (RTO) Achievement
description: Percentage of disaster recovery tests or actual invocations where the RTO was achieved. Measures readiness for business continuity events.
measurement_guidance: Test at least annually per critical service. Document actual recovery times. Compare to stated RTO targets.

name: Recovery Point Objective (RPO) Achievement
description: Percentage of recovery tests or actual invocations where data loss was within the agreed RPO. Measures backup and replication effectiveness.
measurement_guidance: Verify data currency after recovery. Check backup completion status daily. Test restore procedures regularly.

name: DR Test Frequency and Success Rate
description: Frequency of disaster recovery tests conducted and the percentage that pass all acceptance criteria. Measures continuity readiness.
measurement_guidance: Define pass/fail criteria before each test. Document all issues found. Track remediation of test findings.

name: Backup Success Rate
description: Percentage of scheduled backup jobs that complete successfully. Critical for ensuring data recovery capability.
measurement_guidance: Monitor daily. Alert on any failed backups. Test restore from backups regularly. Track by system.
